headlines.summary: August WTI crude oil traded higher during the week ending July 10, with prices last at $71.84 through Thursday evening, up $3.38, or 4.94%. headlines.title: ADNOC Orders $900 Million in New LNG Carriers to Expand Global Fleet
headlines.summary: ADNOC Logistics and Services has placed a $900-million order for four newbuild LNG carriers to expand its fleet as Abu Dhabi’s national oil company seeks to boost gas exports to seize the global rise in LNG demand. headlines.title: Fuel Refining Margins Hit Record Highs as Markets Tighten
headlines.summary: Refining margins for gasoline and diesel jumped this week to new record highs after the re-escalation in the Middle East, Russia’s ban on diesel exports, and crumbling global fuel inventories. headlines.title: More LNG Carriers Brave the Strait of Hormuz Despite Renewed Hostilities
headlines.summary: Close to half a dozen liquefied natural gas carriers have gone into the Strait of Hormuz in the past few days and six have exited the strait, despite the recent return to hostilities between the United States and Iran. headlines.title: India Expands Strategic Oil Reserves With New ONGC Storage Plan
headlines.summary: India’s ONGC plans to build up a strategic national reserve of crude totalling 1.75 million tons, or 12.8 million barrels. headlines.title: Oil Prices Set for Weekly Gain as Hormuz Tensions Escalate
headlines.summary: Crude oil prices were on course for a weekly gain today as hostilities between the United States and Iran flared up again earlier in the week. headlines.title: People Are Talking About Contango While Oil Markets Are Far From Recovered
headlines.summary: While financial desks obsess over paper structures and Brent crude briefly flirted with contango, the broader market is missing the forest for the trees. headlines.title: Fed Sees Oil Prices Cooling Despite Renewed Iran War
headlines.summary: The Federal Reserve still thinks energy prices are headed lower even as missiles are flying again in the Middle East. headlines.title: Turkey and Iraq Move to Keep Critical Oil Export Route Alive
headlines.summary: Iraq just bought itself another year.
